CREAMY MUSHROOM PASTA

This rustic pasta dish hails from the forests of Central Europe, where wild mushrooms have been foraged for centuries.
The earthy umami flavors of mushrooms create a deeply satisfying sauce without any cream—though heavy cream is often
added in modern variations. This version balances simplicity with elegance, using butter, garlic, and a touch of white
wine to coax maximum flavor from humble button and portobello mushrooms. The pasta water's starch acts as a natural
emulsifier, creating a silky sauce that coats each strand perfectly. Serve this when you want to impress without fuss.

INGREDIENTS:
450g mixed mushrooms (button, portobello, cremini), sliced
400g fresh pappardelle or tagliatelle pasta
3 cloves garlic, minced
1 large shallot, finely diced
200ml dry white wine
150ml heavy cream
100g butter
50g parmesan, finely grated
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tbsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tsp dried)
2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
Salt and cracked black pepper
Juice of half a lemon
Nutmeg, pinch

METHOD:
1. Clean mushrooms with a damp paper towel. Slice uniformly so they cook evenly.

2. Bring large pot of salted water to boil for pasta. Cook pasta according to package directions until al dente.
Reserve 200ml pasta water before draining.

3. While pasta cooks, heat oil and butter in a large skillet over high heat. Add mushrooms in a single layer.
Do not stir for 3-4 minutes—let them develop a golden crust. Then stir and cook another 5 minutes until golden.

4. Reduce heat to medium. Add shallot and garlic, cook gently for 2 minutes until fragrant. Season with salt and pepper.

5. Deglaze pan with white wine, scraping up any browned bits. Simmer until wine reduces by half (about 3 minutes).

6. Stir in cream and thyme. Simmer gently for 5 minutes. The sauce should thicken slightly.

7. Remove from heat. Stir in parmesan, lemon juice, and a pinch of nutmeg. Taste and adjust seasoning.

8. Add cooked pasta directly to the mushroom cream sauce. Toss gently, adding pasta water as needed to achieve
silky consistency. The sauce should coat the pasta lightly, not pool at bottom of pan.

9. Divide into serving bowls. Garnish with fresh parsley and additional parmesan. Drizzle with quality olive oil.

SERVING: Pair with a crisp white wine. A fresh green salad cuts the richness beautifully.

NOTES: Never wash mushrooms under running water—they absorb moisture. Keep heat moderate to prevent cream from breaking.
For vegetarian version, ensure pasta is egg-free.
